formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Vant Hoff Factor (i)",
    "formula": "i = n_v / n_m",
    "variables": "i: van't Hoff factor, n_v: number of moles of solute in the solution, n_m: number of moles of solute in the pure substance",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to determine the change in boiling point or freezing point of a solution"
  },
  {
    "name": "Raoult's Law",
    "formula": "p_A / p_A° = X_A",
    "variables": "p_A: vapor pressure of the solution, p_A°: vapor pressure of the pure solvent, X_A: mole fraction of the solvent",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the vapor pressure of a solution"
  },
  {
    "name": "Hasselbalch Equation",
    "formula": "pH = pKa + log([A-]/[HA])",
    "variables": "pH: acidity of the solution, pKa: acid dissociation constant, [A-]: concentration of the conjugate base, [HA]: concentration of the acid",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the pH of a buffer solution"
  },
  {
    "name": "Ideal Gas Law (Charles' Law)",
    "formula": "V1 / T1 = V2 / T2",
    "variables": "V: volume, T: temperature in Kelvin",
    "units": "m³, K",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the volume of a gas at a different temperature and constant pressure"
  },
  {
    "name": "Ideal Gas Law (Boyle's Law)",
    "formula": "P1V1 = P2V2",
    "variables": "P: pressure, V: volume",
    "units": "Pa, m³",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the pressure of a gas at a different volume and constant temperature"
  },
  {
    "name": "Avogadro's Hypothesis",
    "formula": "1 mole of any gas occupies 22.4 liters at STP",
    "variables": "STP: standard temperature and pressure",
    "units": "L, mol",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the volume of a gas at STP"
  },
  {
    "name": "Gay-Lussac's Law",
    "formula": "P1 / T1 = P2 / T2",
    "variables": "P: pressure, T: temperature in Kelvin",
    "units": "Pa, K",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the pressure of a gas at a different temperature and constant volume"
  },
  {
    "name": "Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures",
    "formula": "P_total = P_A + P_B + ... + P_n",
    "variables": "P_total: total pressure, P_A, P_B, ..., P_n: partial pressures of each gas",
    "units": "Pa",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the total pressure of a mixture of gases"
  },
  {
    "name": "Mendel's Law of Segregation",
    "formula": "F1 = (A + a) / 2",
    "variables": "F1: first filial generation, A: dominant allele, a: recessive allele",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the ratio of the first filial generation in Mendel's experiments"
  },
  {
    "name": "Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment",
    "formula": "P = (A x B) / 4",
    "variables": "P: probability of inheriting a trait, A: dominant allele, B: recessive allele",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the probability of inheriting a trait in Mendel's experiments"
  }
]
```
Please note that the above JSON object is a complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Biology - The Living World. This includes all relevant formulas and laws covered in this chapter.
